methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Candela is a Series C-stage transportation company that has raised €129M+ to date. The private frontier-tech transportation sector comprises seven identified private peers, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Slate Auto, and ZeroAvia, with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The transportation sector contains only three same-sector peers, and filtering by stage, timing, or valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any cohort—below the minimum threshold of three required for robust peer median comparison. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and misalignment with Candela's specific market dynamics and capital intensity.

The narrow peer universe and absence of public market comparables create material constraints on triangulation. A defensible valuation would require alternative approaches such as discounted cash flow analysis (if reliable unit economics and growth projections are available), cost-of-capital benchmarking against the peer set, or revenue multiple analysis grounded in comparable deployment and sales cycles. Key uncertainties include Candela's path to profitability relative to capital intensity, differentiation versus the named peer set, and whether any peer transactions provide transactional data. Without additional financial detail or transaction comps, a quantitative valuation range cannot be supported by the available inputs.
